title: Monday Docs Print View
excerpt: Hello! I am using Monday to build a large, annual review PDF. While some team members may reference the online version of the document, other team members within my department will prefer a printed version. It would be n…

posts.author: nbean (Nicole Bean)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello! I am using Monday to build a large, annual review PDF. While some team members may reference the online version of the document, other team members within my department will prefer a printed version. It would be nice to have a toggle view where I could work in an environment that shows the automatic page breaks on a standard PDF export, rather than relying on the page break widget and constantly previewing the PDF to see if the document is being properly formatted. Thank you!

posts.author: JIvan (Jennifer Ivan)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have been hoping for an improved user experience with regard to page breaks as well. It seems there should be a way for Monday.com engineers to either a) build in a way to adjust the page while in the export preview - eg. drag/manipulate preview in the export preview window, or b) when user chooses to add page break to document, it automatically pops up a window showing what the result would be if exported.

My work around, was to add the widget where I wanted, then use the Print function and save it to a PDF. That worked out well, but it is a pain.
